title: Water-level and discharge monitoring for inland waterways
summary: Hydrological forecasts support navigability planning and load decisions on rivers and canals.
problem: Inland-waterway operators depend on adequate water depth, which varies with discharge and drought. Low water forces reduced loading or halts navigation, while floods close waterways — both with major logistics impacts that are hard to anticipate.
solution: CEMS flood-awareness systems (EFAS/GloFAS) provide discharge forecasts, while CLMS water-and-wetness products track surface-water extent. Together they support navigability planning, load optimisation and early warning for low-water and flood conditions.

copernicusRole.detail: CEMS discharge forecasts (EFAS) and CLMS water products are combined and interpreted for navigability and loading. Copernicus provides hydrological inputs that require interpretation for the corridor.
